methodology: SPIN Selling + Sandler Up-Front Contracts + Challenger Insights
mandatory_flow: OBSERVATION → PERMISSION → 1-LINE DIAGNOSIS → TINY WIN → QUESTION
pitch_rule: No pitching until prospect answers at least 2 discovery questions

1_hook.name: Hook
1_hook.success_criteria: They reply with context about their situation
guidelines: Reference something specific from latest non-pinned post
Match their energy (Tier A or Tier B)
End with a soft permission question

2_discovery.name: Discovery
2_discovery.success_criteria: You can describe their problem better than them
guidelines: Let them talk more than you

3_diagnosis.name: Diagnosis
guidelines: Be specific - reference actual things you saw
Show the gap, don't pitch the solution yet

4_tiny_win.name: Tiny Win
guidelines: Wait until you have 2+ facts about their situation
Make it relevant to what they told you

5_offer.name: Offer
guidelines: Lead with proof period (10 free calls)

6_call_transition.name: Call Transition
guidelines: Give explicit permission to say no

questions.text: How are you finding clients right now?
Have you signed paying clients yet, or still building?
What's your offer look like — 1:1, group, something else?
What's been the hardest part of getting clients so far?
If you had 5 qualified calls this week, could you take them?
Where are most of your current clients coming from?
What's your close rate on calls right now?
Is the bottleneck getting enough calls or closing them?
How much time are you spending on finding clients vs coaching?
What have you tried for client acquisition that didn't work?
If call volume wasn't an issue, how many clients could you handle?
What's your timeline for wanting this solved?
What does your current acquisition system look like?
Are you looking to add another channel or replace something?
What's your target for new clients per month?
What's worked best for you historically?
What would make this worth your time to explore?
Who else is involved in this decision?
What's your budget range for client acquisition?
If we delivered, what does success look like to you?

reflect_back_prompts: So if I'm hearing you right — [summary]. That accurate?
Sounds like the main thing is [X]. Am I getting that right?

path_a_volume.name: They're getting some clients, want more
path_a_volume.angle: You've got a system that works — we'd add fuel to it

path_b_conversion.name: They're posting but nothing's converting
path_b_conversion.angle: Your content is doing the hard part — there's no capture system

path_c_trust.name: They've tried other services and got burned
path_c_trust.angle: I get it. What specifically didn't work?

mini_audit_examples.name: Content-to-Call Gap
mini_audit_examples.script: Your engagement is solid — comments, questions, shares. But I don't see a clear path from 'interested' to 'booked.' That gap is where people drop off.

mini_audit_examples.name: CTA Mismatch
mini_audit_examples.script: Your CTA is 'DM me [keyword]' but your content attracts people who aren't ready to commit. The interested-but-not-sure crowd needs a softer entry point.

mini_audit_examples.name: Profile Funnel Leak
mini_audit_examples.script: Your bio says [X], link goes to [Y]. Cold visitors see a disconnect. You're probably only converting the most motivated 10%.

mini_audit_examples.name: Engagement Without Capture
mini_audit_examples.script: You respond to comments but don't initiate with people who engage. The likers-who-don't-comment — those are warm leads with no one reaching out.

mini_audit_examples.name: Competitor Gap
mini_audit_examples.script: Coaches in your niche with similar engagement are booking more calls. Difference is someone's actively qualifying their engaged followers.

teach_moments.name: Content Myth
teach_moments.script: Most coaches think the fix is more content. But I've seen mid content outperform bangers. The difference isn't the content — it's what happens after someone sees it.

teach_moments.name: Active vs Passive
teach_moments.script: Your acquisition is passive — post and hope. Coaches who scale have active acquisition — someone initiating with interested people and qualifying them. Fishing vs hunting.

teach_moments.name: Qualification Gap
teach_moments.script: When someone reaches out cold, you don't know if they're ready, can afford it, or are just curious. If someone qualifies first, every call is someone already sold on coaching.

teach_moments.name: Time Math
teach_moments.script: How much time weekly on content, DMs, finding clients? [Let them answer]. That's time not coaching. Most coaches spend 60% on acquisition. What if you flipped it?

teach_moments.name: Show-Up Problem
teach_moments.script: No-shows happen because they booked out of curiosity. When someone confirms intent first, show-up goes from 50% to 80%+. Calls that happen close better.

standard.name: Standard Version
standard.script:
Here's what I'm thinking: 15-20 minutes. I'll ask about your current setup, show you how this would work for you, and you can ask whatever.

At the end, one of three things: it makes sense and we move forward, it doesn't and we part as friends, or you need more info and we figure that out.

Fair?

skeptical.name: Skeptical Lead Version
skeptical.script:
Let's keep it simple: 15 minutes. I'll ask about your situation, you ask about mine. If there's no fit, I'll tell you — and you should tell me too. No pressure.

Sound fair?

busy.name: Busy Lead Version
busy.script:
I know you're busy: 15 minutes max. Quick questions, quick answers, we'll both know by the end if it makes sense. If not, we end early.

Cool?

no_is_okay_phrases: If it's not a fit, that's fine — we'll both know.
This isn't a convince-you call. It's a figure-out-fit call.
If you get on and it's not for you, just say so. No hard feelings.

fu1_48h.name: FU1 (48 hours)
templates: One thing I noticed on your profile — [specific observation]. Does that match what you're seeing, or is it something else?
Thought about what you mentioned. [One insight]. Is that landing, or is your situation different?

fu2_5d.name: FU2 (5 days after FU1)
templates: Worked with a [similar niche] coach recently — [brief result]. Not sure if that's relevant for you. Still thinking about this, or did you go a different direction?

fu3_10d.name: FU3 (10 days after FU2)
templates: I'll let you be — you know your situation best. If the client thing becomes a priority, I'm around. Good luck with [something specific about their work].

follow_up_rules: Every follow-up adds value or asks a genuine question
A/B questions give easy reply options
No guilt language ('guess you're not interested')
If they reply at any point, restart fresh
fit_check_questions: Are you actively signing new clients right now?
If I booked 10 qualified calls this month, could you take them?
What's your current close rate on sales calls?
What do you charge for your main offer?
Who makes the decision to move forward — just you?
fit_check_exit: Sounds like timing might be off. Happy to reconnect in [X weeks] if things shift.
